memoryArtifacts.headline: Is she Mabel or Margaret
memoryArtifacts.description: It is likely that Sir John de Coggeshall's wife was Mabel, daughter of John de Staunton of Alresford, and sister and heiress to Anfrid de Staunton."

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Staunton於大約1310年出生在英國英格蘭艾可時，她的父親John de Staunton32歲，母親Mrs John de Staunton32歲。她於1324年與John de Coggeshall在英國英格蘭艾可布萊崔結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1342年在她的家鄉享年34歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Staunton于大约1310年出生在英国英格兰艾可时，她的父亲John de Staunton32岁，母亲Mrs John de Staunton32岁。她于1324年与John de Coggeshall在英国英格兰艾可布萊崔结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1342年在她的家乡享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Staunton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Caitlin, Ciaran, Cormac.

English and Irish: variant of Stanton . This English name has been established (in this spelling) since medieval times in Ireland (Mayo, Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as de Stonndún. Compare McEvilly .

variants.name: McEvilly
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Mhílidh ‘son of the knight’, from Gaelic míleadh ‘warrior, knight’. In County Mayo and County Cork, a family of English origin Gaelicized their name from Staunton to Mac an Mhílidh, apparently because of the latter's presumed similarity to the name of their ancestor, Milo de Stanton.

variants.name: Stanton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in England called Stanton or Staunton, especially Stanton in Staffordshire and Staunton in Worcestershire, all named with Old English stān ‘stone’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Most of the placenames come from their situation on stony ground, but in the case of Stanton Harcourt in Oxfordshire and Stanton Drew in Avon the reference is to the proximity of prehistoric stone monuments. The name has also sometimes been chosen by Ashkenazic Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames. This surname has also long been established in Ireland (especially Mayo).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Staunton was born about 1310, in Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, John de Staunton, was 32 and her mother, Mrs John de Staunton, was 32. She married John de Coggeshall in 1324, in Braintree, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. She died about 1342, in her hometown, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Staunton est née environ 1310, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Staunton, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mrs John de Staunton, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé John de Coggeshall en 1324, à Braintree, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1342, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Staunton um 1310 in Essex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Staunton 32 und ihre Mutter Mrs John de Staunton 32. Sie hat 1324 in Braintree, Essex, England, United Kingdom John de Coggeshall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb um 1342 im Alter von 34 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Staunton è nata il circa 1310 nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John de Staunton, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Mrs John de Staunton, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato John de Coggeshall nel 1324, nel Braintree, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1342, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 34 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Stauntonが約1310年にイギリスイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父John de Stauntonは32歳，母Mrs John de Stauntonは32歳であった。1324年にイギリスイギリスエセックスブレイントレでJohn de Coggeshallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1342年に故郷で34歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Staunton(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Staunton(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs John de Staunton(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1324년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 브레이트리에서 John de Coggeshall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1342년 그녀의 고향에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Staunton nasceu aproximadamente 1310, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Staunton, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Mrs John de Staunton, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com John de Coggeshall em 1324, em Braintree, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1342, em sua cidade natal, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Staunton родилась около 1310 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John de Staunton было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs John de Staunton, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Coggeshall в 1324 г. в местности бреинтри, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1342 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Staunton nació aproximadamente 1310, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Staunton, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Mrs John de Staunton, tenía 32 años. Se casó con John de Coggeshall en 1324, en Braintree, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1342, en su pueblo, a la edad de 34 años.
